title: The One With Ross's Wedding
weakest_section: 45:00-47:00 range with ceremony buildup having fewer jokes

jokes.text: Hurry up! The flight leaves in four hours! There could be traffic! The plane could leave early! In London, there could be a line at customs! Come on!
jokes.setup: Monica rushing everyone for a flight in four hours
jokes.punchline: Each excuse becomes more absurd, especially planes leaving early
jokes.explanation: Perfect Monica neurosis - her anxiety manifests as increasingly irrational concerns about travel timing

jokes.text: Six-hour trip to London. That's a lot of Monica.
jokes.setup: Long flight with Monica who just demonstrated her travel anxiety
jokes.punchline: Understated acknowledgment of Monica's intensity
jokes.explanation: Classic Chandler deadpan observation about a friend's quirks

jokes.text: What have you got there? Condoms, dude. Well, how come the writing is in spanish? Don't read my condoms.
jokes.setup: Ross notices Joey has condoms
jokes.punchline: Joey's defensive 'Don't read my condoms' after being caught with Spanish ones
jokes.explanation: Funny defensive line with absurd privacy boundary about condom reading

jokes.text: -Who are you saying 'check' to? -Myself. For remembering to pack a thing. You do a good thing, you get a 'check.'
jokes.setup: Monica doing travel checklist aloud
jokes.punchline: Her bizarre self-reward system of saying 'check' to herself
jokes.explanation: Monica's compulsive behavior presented as a quirky reward system

jokes.text: Mom does it. I never realized it was weird. My mom used to put her head in the oven.
jokes.setup: Monica defending her 'check' behavior because mom does it
jokes.punchline: Ross's casual mention of mom putting her head in oven
jokes.explanation: Excellent misdirection - comparing harmless quirk to something much darker

jokes.text: Actually, only the one time. But it's pretty weird.
jokes.setup: Previous comment about mom putting head in oven
jokes.punchline: Ross clarifying it was only once, still calling it 'pretty weird'
jokes.explanation: Nice follow-up that maintains the dark humor while downplaying it

jokes.text: Got your passport? Third drawer of my dresser. You don't want to lose that!
jokes.setup: Monica asking Ross about his passport for international travel
jokes.punchline: Ross admits it's in his dresser drawer at home, not with him
jokes.explanation: Standard absent-minded character humor, predictable setup

jokes.text: Let's do it. Cheerio! London, baby! That's not gonna get annoying. London, baby! You know what? I was wrong.
jokes.setup: Joey enthusiastically saying 'London, baby!' catchphrase
jokes.punchline: Chandler sarcastically predicting it won't get annoying, proven immediately wrong
jokes.explanation: Sets up running gag while immediately showing its annoying nature

jokes.text: I want to go over and hug you and wish you luck, but I can't get up. -I'll come hug you. -Great, can you bring me the paper?
jokes.setup: Phoebe wanting to hug Monica goodbye but can't get up
jokes.punchline: Immediately asking Monica to bring the newspaper while she's at it
jokes.explanation: Good pregnant Phoebe humor - taking advantage of offered help

jokes.text: Chandler, I want to hug you too! You might as well bring my book from your apartment.
jokes.setup: Following the previous newspaper request pattern
jokes.punchline: Phoebe now asking Chandler to bring a book from his apartment
jokes.explanation: Continues the pattern but with diminishing returns

jokes.text: Here you go, Phoebe! Phoe-bo! Phoo-ba-la!
jokes.setup: Joey saying goodbye to Phoebe
jokes.punchline: His increasingly silly variations on her name
jokes.explanation: Simple Joey-esque name play, not particularly clever

jokes.text: London, baby!
jokes.setup: Continuation of Joey's catchphrase
jokes.punchline: Repetition of the annoying phrase
jokes.explanation: Running gag maintenance, becomes funnier through repetition

jokes.text: Oh, God! Do you need a hug? You don't have to bring me anything.
jokes.setup: Phoebe alone after everyone left for London
jokes.punchline: Her immediate emotional shift and joke about not needing anything brought
jokes.explanation: Nice emotional beat with callback to her earlier requests

jokes.text: Do something! I am. I'm ignoring you.
jokes.setup: Monica demanding Chandler do something about their situation
jokes.punchline: Chandler's literal interpretation that ignoring is doing something
jokes.explanation: Perfect Chandler response - technically correct while being unhelpful

jokes.text: If you see a little version of me in there, kill it!
jokes.setup: Joey looking at a map and getting confused by his location
jokes.punchline: His absurd fear of being trapped inside the map
jokes.explanation: Wonderfully absurd Joey logic about maps - highly quotable and memorable

jokes.text: Well, up yours too! -What? -Oh, that's not what it means. -No! That's 'time out.' -Sorry.
jokes.setup: Emily making a British gesture Ross doesn't understand
jokes.punchline: Ross thinking it's an insult and Emily explaining it's time out
jokes.explanation: Standard cultural misunderstanding humor, well-executed but predictable

jokes.text: And you wouldn't worry about the salmonella. But of course...with salmon you would have to worry about the chicknella.
jokes.setup: Ross comparing chicken and salmon for catering
jokes.punchline: His made-up word 'chicknella' as salmon's equivalent to salmonella
jokes.explanation: Clever wordplay that sounds plausible enough to be funny

jokes.text: They tore it down early.
jokes.setup: The wedding venue being demolished
jokes.punchline: The simple, understated explanation for the crisis
jokes.explanation: Good deadpan delivery of terrible news

jokes.text: Hands down, best abbey I ever seen.
jokes.setup: Joey at Westminster Abbey
jokes.punchline: His overly casual review as if he's seen many abbeys
jokes.explanation: Typical Joey treating everything like a casual experience

jokes.text: It's great! They're thinking of changing the name. -To what? -To 'Put the Camera Away'!
jokes.setup: Joey asking what Chandler thinks of the Abbey
jokes.punchline: Chandler's sarcastic suggestion about Joey's constant filming
jokes.explanation: Clever way to address Joey's tourist behavior through fake renaming

jokes.text: Man, you are Westminster Crabby.
jokes.setup: Chandler being irritable at Westminster Abbey
jokes.punchline: Joey's pun combining Westminster with 'crabby'
jokes.explanation: Decent pun that fits Joey's style of obvious wordplay

jokes.text: Sorry. Lucky this dress is red.
jokes.setup: Monica dealing with wedding crisis stress
jokes.punchline: Acknowledging she's probably spilled or stained her dress
jokes.explanation: Practical Monica observation about dress color and accidents

jokes.text: What should be the most wonderful day of my life is rapidly turning into just Sunday with a spectacular amount of food.
jokes.setup: Emily's wedding falling apart
jokes.punchline: Reducing wedding to 'Sunday with spectacular amount of food'
jokes.explanation: Excellent reframing of wedding disaster - specific and relatable

jokes.text: Okay, I have a wedgie. That is all you.
jokes.setup: Rachel offering to help pregnant Phoebe with anything
jokes.punchline: Phoebe's uncomfortable request and Rachel's immediate refusal
jokes.explanation: Good physical comedy setup with appropriate boundary-setting

jokes.text: Don't be so hard on yourself. If I was still in love with some— Still in love with? I'm not in love with Ross.
jokes.setup: Phoebe accidentally revealing Rachel's feelings
jokes.punchline: Rachel's shocked reaction and immediate denial
jokes.explanation: Standard sitcom moment of accidental revelation

jokes.text: I have sexual feelings for him, but I do love him. Oh, my God!
jokes.setup: Rachel trying to rationalize her feelings for Ross
jokes.punchline: Her logic leading her to realize she does love him
jokes.explanation: Good escalation as Rachel talks herself into the realization

jokes.text: It would be like telling Monica: 'Hey, you like things clean.' Or, you know, 'Hey, Joey, you're gay.'
jokes.setup: Phoebe explaining how obvious Rachel's feelings are
jokes.punchline: Comparing it to obvious facts, then saying Joey's gay
jokes.explanation: Unexpected third example that creates confusion about Joey

jokes.text: What? Oh, please, she's always got a broom in her hand.
jokes.setup: Rachel questioning the Joey comment
jokes.punchline: Phoebe clarifying she meant Monica with the cleaning reference
jokes.explanation: Good callback clarification that resolves the confusion

jokes.text: I won't need that 'I'm with stupid' T-shirt anymore.
jokes.setup: Chandler seeing Joey's ridiculous tourist hat
jokes.punchline: Implying the hat itself identifies Joey as stupid
jokes.explanation: Sharp Chandler insult about Joey's fashion choices

jokes.text: How can I answer when I'm pretending I don't know you?
jokes.setup: Joey asking why Chandler won't respond to his embarrassment
jokes.punchline: Chandler explaining he's disowning Joey publicly
jokes.explanation: Good Chandler logic about social embarrassment

jokes.text: If you make me choose between you and the hat, I choose the hat.
jokes.setup: Chandler threatening to leave Joey alone if he wears the hat
jokes.punchline: Joey choosing the hat over his friend
jokes.explanation: Perfect Joey priority system - fashion over friendship

jokes.text: How are you doing? Joey Tribbiani, America.
jokes.setup: Joey trying to pick up British woman
jokes.punchline: His awkward introduction representing his entire country
jokes.explanation: Classic Joey pickup line with patriotic twist

jokes.text: Do you think my sister's teeny, tiny, little brain comprehends...that people took time out of their lives...to fly thousands of miles to be here?
jokes.setup: Ross angry at Monica's suggestion to postpone wedding
jokes.punchline: His condescending description of Monica's 'teeny, tiny, little brain'
jokes.explanation: Ross's frustrated insult shows his stress and sibling dynamics

jokes.text: Don't you point your pants at me!
jokes.setup: Ross having wardrobe malfunction during argument
jokes.punchline: His bizarre command about pointing pants
jokes.explanation: Wonderfully absurd line that became iconic - physical comedy meets verbal nonsense

jokes.text: Manhattan doesn't have enough stores.
jokes.setup: Phoebe asking if shopping helped Rachel feel better
jokes.punchline: Rachel's impossible complaint about Manhattan shopping options
jokes.explanation: Good Rachel materialism joke - Manhattan obviously has tons of stores

jokes.text: Only if you have the hiccups too.
jokes.setup: Rachel asking if Phoebe's ritual involves graveyard dirt and trees
jokes.punchline: Phoebe's casual addition that hiccups would require that
jokes.explanation: Perfect Phoebe weirdness - treating magical thinking as practical

jokes.text: The chocolate and water are for me. I didn't feel like getting up.
jokes.setup: Elaborate ritual setup with chocolate and water
jokes.punchline: Revealing they're just snacks she wanted
jokes.explanation: Nice deflation of mystical setup - pure pregnant laziness

jokes.text: His hair never bothered me that much. It was more crunchy than it was greasy.
jokes.setup: Phoebe trying to make Rachel focus on Ross's flaws
jokes.punchline: Rachel's detailed description making his hair sound worse
jokes.explanation: Good gross-out humor that reveals Rachel's love despite flaws

jokes.text: Well, I like you less.
jokes.setup: Phoebe's aversion therapy shocking Rachel
jokes.punchline: Rachel's understated response to being shocked
jokes.explanation: Good understatement after presumably being shocked

jokes.text: Me too. -Yeah? -No! I had the best day ever!
jokes.setup: Chandler saying he had a lousy day, Joey agreeing
jokes.punchline: Joey immediately contradicting himself with enthusiasm
jokes.explanation: Classic Joey confusion and enthusiasm overriding empathy

jokes.text: That's Fergie, baby!
jokes.setup: Joey meeting someone famous in London
jokes.punchline: His excited celebrity name-drop with his catchphrase
jokes.explanation: Basic celebrity encounter humor with catchphrase callback

jokes.text: Oh, my God! You're even dumber than I am.
jokes.setup: Ross explaining what he said to Emily about postponing
jokes.punchline: Monica's self-deprecating insult about Ross's stupidity
jokes.explanation: Monica's rare self-awareness used to insult Ross

jokes.text: That's what we did. We dreamed about the perfect wedding. In the perfect place. With the perfect four-tiered wedding cake. With the little people on top.
jokes.setup: Monica explaining how girls dream about weddings
jokes.punchline: Her specific detail about 'little people on top' of cake
jokes.explanation: Monica's wedding obsession with oddly specific cake detail

jokes.text: That pillowcase thing, I thought you were doing The Flying Nun. Sometimes we were.
jokes.setup: Ross admitting he misunderstood Monica's wedding dress play
jokes.punchline: Monica casually admitting they sometimes were playing Flying Nun
jokes.explanation: Nice callback that adds childhood detail and humor

jokes.text: I'll get it. Pheebs, it's Joey. Hey, Joey! I saw someone that looks just like you on the subway. I was going to say hi, then I figured he doesn't care he looks like you.
jokes.setup: Phoebe seeing someone who looks like Joey
jokes.punchline: Her logic that the person wouldn't care about resembling Joey
jokes.explanation: Good Phoebe logic about celebrity doppelgangers

jokes.text: Who's seven? Me, Rachel...the birds, the babies. The babies miss me? Of course they do, or I'm just really hungry.
jokes.setup: Joey saying seven people miss him
jokes.punchline: Phoebe's uncertainty about whether babies miss him or she's hungry
jokes.explanation: Perfect pregnant Phoebe confusion about internal sensations

jokes.text: Two pizzas? -Gotta go! Talk to you later. -Wait! Where did you get it from?
jokes.setup: Joey Special pizza order revealed as two pizzas
jokes.punchline: Joey hanging up immediately, Phoebe desperately asking location
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Joey's food priorities and Phoebe's pregnancy cravings

jokes.text: Then we'll get wet. I don't even have a date.
jokes.setup: Ross saying if it rains at outdoor wedding they'll get wet
jokes.punchline: Monica's unrelated complaint about not having a date
jokes.explanation: Monica's self-centered response to wedding logistics

jokes.text: You're going because you hope he'll say: 'I love you too. Forget the British chippy.'
jokes.setup: Rachel claiming she just wants Ross to have all the information
jokes.punchline: Phoebe calling out Rachel's real motivation and calling Emily 'British chippy'
jokes.explanation: Phoebe cutting through Rachel's rationalization with British slang

jokes.text: No, it's not over until someone says, 'I do.' I do! I do!
jokes.setup: Rachel saying it's not over until someone says I do
jokes.punchline: Phoebe immediately saying I do to end the conversation
jokes.explanation: Clever use of Rachel's own logic against her

jokes.text: Like I can really chase you. I'm carrying a litter!
jokes.setup: Rachel leaving despite Phoebe's objections
jokes.punchline: Phoebe referring to her pregnancy as carrying a litter
jokes.explanation: Perfect Phoebe animal comparison for her brother's babies

jokes.text: First one identifies oneself...and then asks for the person with whom one wishes to speak. What are you saying? Now, let us try that again, shall we? No! Oh, my God!
jokes.setup: Proper British housekeeper correcting Phoebe's phone etiquette
jokes.punchline: Phoebe's American directness clashing with British formality
jokes.explanation: Good culture clash between American directness and British propriety

jokes.text: So if you don't give me that number, I'm gonna come over there...and I'm gonna kick your snooty ass all the way to New Gloukenshire!
jokes.setup: Phoebe getting frustrated with British housekeeper
jokes.punchline: Her threat involving made-up British place name
jokes.explanation: Creative fake British place name in Phoebe's threat

jokes.text: She knew I could kick her ass.
jokes.setup: Housekeeper hanging up on Phoebe
jokes.punchline: Phoebe's confident interpretation of why she hung up
jokes.explanation: Phoebe's delusional confidence about intimidating someone over phone

jokes.text: Jack, that's what they call the subway. -I thought -Dad, we got it.
jokes.setup: Jack Geller explaining London terminology
jokes.punchline: Ross cutting off his dad's obvious explanation
jokes.explanation: Standard parent embarrassment humor

jokes.text: She's very self-involved. I should never have married her. -It's the Gellers. -Where? That's one and that's another one.
jokes.setup: Steven calling Andrea self-involved
jokes.punchline: Andrea immediately proving it by needing the Gellers pointed out
jokes.explanation: Immediate demonstration of the character flaw just mentioned

jokes.text: A joke that's funny in all countries.
jokes.setup: Jack Geller's joke about only wedding/grandchildren
jokes.punchline: Ross's sarcastic comment about universal humor
jokes.explanation: Meta comment about cross-cultural comedy

jokes.text: That's not food.
jokes.setup: Waiter describing fancy British appetizer
jokes.punchline: Joey's direct rejection of fancy food description
jokes.explanation: Perfect Joey reaction to pretentious food - simple and direct

jokes.text: I can't even remember what Phoebe looks like!
jokes.setup: Chandler homesick and missing familiar things
jokes.punchline: Claiming he can't remember Phoebe after three days
jokes.explanation: Chandler's dramatic exaggeration of short-term memory loss

jokes.text: You're mean in England!
jokes.setup: Monica calling Chandler on his attitude
jokes.punchline: Chandler's accusation that Monica changes personality abroad
jokes.explanation: Chandler deflecting criticism with geographical blame

jokes.text: How about five? Thank you.
jokes.setup: Rachel negotiating down from $2700 to $600 for plane ticket
jokes.punchline: Her final desperate offer of five cards to split the fare
jokes.explanation: Standard desperate traveler humor about payment methods

jokes.text: And I have a 20.
jokes.setup: Rachel unable to provide passport for international flight
jokes.punchline: Her pathetic attempt to bribe with twenty dollars
jokes.explanation: Rachel's desperation leading to inappropriate bribery attempt

jokes.text: Why am I always pregnant when she does that?
jokes.setup: Rachel leaving suddenly while Phoebe can't follow
jokes.punchline: Phoebe's complaint about timing of pregnancy with friend drama
jokes.explanation: Good Phoebe observation about inconvenient timing patterns

jokes.text: I thought things were going to work out for him, until the day he over-inflated her.
jokes.setup: Chandler making toast about Ross's first girlfriend
jokes.punchline: Revealing the girlfriend was a blow-up doll
jokes.explanation: Excellent dark humor with perfect setup - sounds like relationship advice until the punchline

jokes.text: I got it from your maid. She's a bitch, but I wore her down.
jokes.setup: Mrs. Waltham asking how Phoebe got the number
jokes.punchline: Phoebe's casual insult and admission of persistence
jokes.explanation: Phoebe's blunt assessment of her phone battle victory

jokes.text: I can't fly. I'm having my brother's babies. Am I on the radio?
jokes.setup: Phoebe explaining why she's not at the wedding
jokes.punchline: Her confusion about whether she's being broadcast
jokes.explanation: Phoebe's weird explanation and confusion about phone call format

jokes.text: To think, my friend getting married in Monty Hall. Come on, Monty Hall! Let's Make a Deal. Come on, you people!
jokes.setup: Chandler hearing Montgomery Hall wedding venue name
jokes.punchline: His game show host impression and frustration at audience not getting it
jokes.explanation: Chandler's pop culture reference that falls flat with British audience

jokes.text: But not as much as I love America.
jokes.setup: Joey making wedding toast saying he loves Ross and Emily
jokes.punchline: His qualifier that he loves America more
jokes.explanation: Perfect Joey patriotism interrupting romantic moment

jokes.text: Out loud? I didn't want everyone to think I was stupid.
jokes.setup: Someone asking Monica if she was laughing out loud
jokes.punchline: Her admission she was fake laughing to appear smart
jokes.explanation: Monica's social anxiety about appearing intelligent at formal event

jokes.text: My God! You must have been a teenager when you had him.
jokes.setup: British man complimenting Judy Geller
jokes.punchline: His over-the-top flattery about her age when she had Ross
jokes.explanation: Standard older person flattery humor

jokes.text: you thieving, would-be-speaking-German-if-it-weren't-for-us, cheap little man!
jokes.setup: Jack arguing with British man about wedding costs
jokes.punchline: His WWII reference as ultimate insult
jokes.explanation: Escalated insult invoking WWII - politically incorrect but memorable

jokes.text: He came up to me earlier and thanked me for my very moving performance in Titanic.
jokes.setup: Monica worried about looking old after British man's comment
jokes.punchline: Chandler's evidence the man is confused about who people are
jokes.explanation: Good way to deflate Monica's concern about the previous compliment

jokes.text: Please! I'm a single mom with a 30-year-old son.
jokes.setup: Chandler asking who wouldn't want Monica
jokes.punchline: Monica's self-deprecating list of perceived flaws
jokes.explanation: Monica's realistic but harsh self-assessment

jokes.text: I'm getting married today!
jokes.setup: Ross excited about his wedding day
jokes.punchline: His enthusiastic repetition and energy
jokes.explanation: Basic wedding day excitement, not particularly clever

jokes.text: Or perhaps slip you one.
jokes.setup: Rachel being loud and excited on plane
jokes.punchline: British passenger's dry suggestion about sedating Rachel
jokes.explanation: Good British dry humor about dealing with annoying passenger

jokes.text: Well, I've never done that with you before.
jokes.setup: Rachel talking to airplane passenger about love declaration
jokes.punchline: Her accidental sexual innuendo to stranger
jokes.explanation: Accidental innuendo humor, fairly predictable

jokes.text: Could you not look? I don't want to look.
jokes.setup: Monica and Chandler in awkward physical situation
jokes.punchline: Both asking each other not to look while both wanting to avoid looking
jokes.explanation: Physical comedy moment with mutual embarrassment

jokes.text: So tell me about this girl.
jokes.setup: Joey just learned Rachel is coming to ruin Ross's wedding
jokes.punchline: His immediate shift to wanting gossip about Chandler's girl
jokes.explanation: Perfect Joey priorities - gossip over crisis management

jokes.text: This is a terrible, terrible plan. -But he has to know how I feel. -But why?
jokes.setup: Rachel explaining her plan to confess love at wedding
jokes.punchline: Stranger giving obvious advice about terrible timing
jokes.explanation: Stranger serving as voice of reason about Rachel's bad plan

jokes.text: And by the way, it seems to be perfectly clear that you were on a break.
jokes.setup: Stranger listening to Rachel's relationship history
jokes.punchline: His definitive judgment on the famous Ross/Rachel break debate
jokes.explanation: Brilliant outside perspective on long-running show debate - highly quotable

jokes.text: What? We gotta find her. Rachel's coming to tell Ross she loves him. -Oh, my God! -I know. Do you know where Monica is? No. What's with the third degree? Why not just shine a light in my eyes?
jokes.setup: Joey asking Chandler where Monica is urgently
jokes.punchline: Chandler's defensive overreaction to simple question
jokes.explanation: Chandler's guilty overreaction suggests he knows exactly where Monica is

jokes.text: You can let some of them go by.
jokes.setup: Monica making multiple comments about wedding hopes
jokes.punchline: Joey's advice that she doesn't need to comment on everything
jokes.explanation: Joey giving Monica advice about conversation restraint

jokes.text: There's nothing to discuss. We're not paying for your wine cellar. You have to meet me in the middle here. My foot's gonna meet the middle of your ass.
jokes.setup: Wedding payment negotiations getting heated
jokes.punchline: Jack's threat using Steven's own 'meet in the middle' language
jokes.explanation: Good wordplay turning diplomatic language into threat

jokes.text: I could kill you with my thumb, you know.
jokes.setup: Jack Geller in confrontation with British man
jokes.punchline: His specific but implausible threat about thumb killing
jokes.explanation: Absurd specific threat that's both menacing and ridiculous

jokes.text: I just came to...I just needed to tell you...congratulations.
jokes.setup: Rachel arriving at wedding ready to confess her love
jokes.punchline: Her decision to say congratulations instead
jokes.explanation: Emotional moment rather than pure comedy - Rachel's sacrifice

jokes.text: I'm at the front with Ross. It's Phoebe! He looks pretty mad. I better go.
jokes.setup: Joey giving Phoebe phone commentary during wedding ceremony
jokes.punchline: His observation that Ross looks mad and need to hang up
jokes.explanation: Joey's inappropriate phone behavior during solemn ceremony

jokes.text: What we did last night was -Stupid. Totally crazy, stupid. What were we thinking? I'm coming over tonight though, right? Definitely.
jokes.setup: Monica and Chandler discussing their night together
jokes.punchline: Calling it stupid while immediately planning to repeat it
jokes.explanation: Perfect contradiction between their words and actions - major plot development

jokes.text: I, Ross...take thee, Emily...take thee, Rachel— Emily.
jokes.setup: Ross saying wedding vows to Emily
jokes.punchline: His massive mistake saying Rachel's name instead
jokes.explanation: Iconic sitcom moment - the ultimate wedding disaster that became cultural touchstone

jokes.text: Shall I go on?
jokes.setup: Ross just said Rachel's name instead of Emily's at altar
jokes.punchline: Minister's understated question about continuing ceremony
jokes.explanation: Perfect understated response to massive awkwardness - economical and memorable

jokes.text: Why, what's the matter?
jokes.setup: Everyone looking shocked after the wedding disaster
jokes.punchline: Joey's oblivious question about what's wrong
jokes.explanation: Classic Joey obliviousness to obvious social disaster
